name: Japanese Spider Crab

visual_attributes.morphology: elongated legs with a relatively small body
distinctive_features: orange color with white spots along the legs
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: leg span up to 3.7 meters (12.1 ft), carapace width up to 40 cm (16 in)
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: long legs radiating from a central body

domain_specific_visual.CHELIPEDS: males have longer chelipeds than females
domain_specific_visual.CAMOUFLAGE: adorns shell with sponges and other organisms

domain_specific_functional.MOLTING_BEHAVIOR: unique molting process lasting about 100 minutes

relationships.description: native to the southern coasts of Honshū, Japan
octopuses are known predators of the Japanese spider crab

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: southern coasts of Honshū, Japan, and parts of Taiwan

contrastive_constraints: NOT tropical or warm water habitat
